pi_items.source: Verified against setup_task.sh (line 9: rm -f /home/ga/Desktop/incident_trace.har), export_result.sh (line 14: HAR_PATH=/home/ga/Desktop/incident_trace.har), and task description (line 6)
Verified against setup_task.sh (line 9), export_result.sh (lines 14, 36), and task description which specifies Desktop (/home/ga/Desktop)

Verified via HTTP HEAD request: returns HTTP 200 with text/html content-type. Also verified in task description (line 6: 'Navigate to https://httpbin.org/forms/post'). Verifier checks for presence of 'httpbin.org/forms/post' in captured URLs (verifier.py line 92)

Verified via HTTP HEAD request: returns HTTP 200 with application/json content-type and introduces ~3 second delay. Task description (line 6: 'Navigate to https://httpbin.org/delay/3'). Verifier checks for presence of 'httpbin.org/delay/3' and validates latency > 2000ms (verifier.py lines 97-101)

Verified via HTTP HEAD request: returns HTTP 503 (Service Unavailable). Task description (line 6: 'Navigate to https://httpbin.org/status/503'). Verifier checks for presence of 'httpbin.org/status/503' and validates response status == 503 (verifier.py lines 104-106)

privileged_info_summary: The task requires capturing network traffic from three real public httpbin.org endpoints: (1) POST to /forms/post which serves an HTML form, (2) /delay/3 which returns JSON with a 3-second delay, and (3) /status/503 which returns HTTP 503 error. The HAR file must be named 'incident_trace.har' and saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/incident_trace.har' and must contain all three network events to verify that 'Preserve log' was enabled in Microsoft Edge Developer Tools. Data is real (from public testing service httpbin.org), not synthetic.
